title: freycinetia kiekie
intro: Freycinetia kiekie, also known as the Kiekie or Ti, is a type of climbing vine native to New Zealand, belonging to the family Pandanaceae. It has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The health benefits of Freycinetia kiekie include soothing skin irritations, reducing fever, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Traditional herbal preparations of Freycinetia kiekie include infusions, poultices, and topical creams, which can be made by boiling the leaves in water or mixing them with other herbs to create a medicinal paste.
